card.indications: Used with other antiretroviral medications to treat HIV-1 infection. It is not used alone.
card.mechanism: Abacavir is converted inside cells to an active carbovir triphosphate metabolite. It competes with the natural building blocks used by HIV reverse transcriptase, causing viral DNA chain termination and reducing HIV replication.
card.dosing: Adults: 600 mg/day by mouth as 300 mg twice daily or 600 mg once daily. Pediatric patients age 3 months and older: weight-based dosing, maximum 600 mg/day. Mild hepatic impairment: 200 mg twice daily using oral solution. Moderate or severe hepatic impairment: contraindicated.
card.routeAdministration: Oral only. Tablets and oral solution may be taken with or without food. Confirm the patient can swallow the tablet; use oral solution when a liquid formulation is needed. Give as part of a complete antiretroviral regimen.
card.contraindications: Positive HLA-B*5701 test; previous hypersensitivity reaction to abacavir; moderate or severe hepatic impairment.
card.warnings: Boxed warning for serious and sometimes fatal multi-organ hypersensitivity. Screen for HLA-B*5701 before starting or restarting. Stop immediately when hypersensitivity is suspected and never restart if it cannot be ruled out. Also monitor for lactic acidosis, severe hepatomegaly with steatosis, immune reconstitution syndrome, and possible cardiovascular risk.
card.commonAdverseEffects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, headache, fatigue or malaise, sleep disturbance or abnormal dreams, rash, abdominal discomfort, and dizziness.
card.seriousAdverseEffects: Fatal hypersensitivity or anaphylaxis; lactic acidosis; severe hepatotoxicity or fatty liver; immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome; Stevens-Johnson syndrome or toxic epidermal necrolysis; hypotension, respiratory failure, renal failure, or liver failure associated with hypersensitivity.
card.interactions: Methadone exposure may decrease, so some patients may need a methadone dose adjustment. Riociguat exposure may increase and may require dose reduction. Review the complete antiretroviral regimen for duplicate abacavir-containing products.
card.monitoring: Before therapy: HLA-B*5701, medication and allergy history, liver function, HIV viral load, and CD4 count. During therapy: assess for hypersensitivity symptoms, adherence, viral load and CD4 response, liver enzymes, signs of lactic acidosis, and symptoms of immune reconstitution. Consider cardiovascular risk factors.
card.patientTeaching: Carry and read the abacavir Warning Card. Report fever, rash, nausea/vomiting/diarrhea or abdominal pain, severe fatigue or achiness, shortness of breath, cough, or sore throat immediately—especially when symptoms occur in two or more groups. Never restart after a suspected hypersensitivity reaction unless specifically directed after medical evaluation. Take exactly as prescribed, do not double missed doses, and do not run out.
card.nutrition: May be taken with or without food. Food does not meaningfully change exposure. Maintain hydration if nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea occurs. Report poor intake, persistent GI symptoms, unusual weakness, or abdominal swelling because these may signal serious toxicity.
